curation_notes: Primary locations derived from the patch diff. In path_oidc.go, the pathCallback function read the error_description query parameter and passed it directly to loginFailedResponse without sanitization, enabling reflected XSS. In html_responses.go, the errorHTML function used fmt.Sprintf to embed summary and detail strings into HTML without html.EscapeString, and formpostHTML embedded path/code/state into a script block using plain string interpolation instead of JSON-safe encoding. The patch adds html.EscapeString for HTML contexts and a jsStr helper (json.Marshal) for script contexts, and replaces raw error_description reflection with a whitelist of known OIDC error codes. No sibling files share this vulnerability pattern. Pre-assigned class xss is correct — CWE-80 (Improper Neutralization of Script-Related HTML Tags) directly maps to XSS.
